promise_statement: The first putter engineered to work WITH gravity — so your putting finally matches the rest of your game.
campaign_thesis: The FIRST putter engineered to work WITH gravity — not against it — so your putting finally matches the rest of your game.

metadata.proof_ceiling_rationale: No independent test results exist yet for RS1. Physics are demonstrable (Let It Fall, FDB), engineering credentials are verified (Chris McGinley), but specific outcome data (strokes gained, accuracy percentages) is pending. Promise must be calibrated to what the physics demonstrably deliver (face angle consistency) and what the design enables (technique assist), not specific outcome numbers.

executive_summary.primary_promise: Do less. Make more. — RS1's Forward Axis Weighting does the work gravity never could, so your putting matches the rest of your game.
executive_summary.campaign_thesis: The FIRST putter engineered to work WITH gravity — not against it — so your putting finally matches the rest of your game.

core_statement.full: RS1's Forward Axis Weighting reverses the physics that have been fighting you on every putt — so your putting finally matches the rest of your game. Do less. Make more.
core_statement.core: Your putting finally matches the rest of your game.

primary_promise_package.promise_type_rationale: Capability: grants the new ability to putt without fighting the equipment. Relief: removes the frustration, anxiety, and putter carousel. Hybrid because RS1 both gives something new (gravity-assisted putting) and removes something old (the unconscious fight against face drift).

emotional_frame.vindication_expression: Your technique was never the problem. The weight was in the wrong place. RS1 moves it forward — and the fight is over.
emotional_frame.freedom_expression: Free from steering the putter face. Free from the practice-green lie. Free from the carousel.
emotional_frame.control_expression: One less variable to think about. The putter handles face control. You handle line and speed.

statement: RS1's Forward Axis Weighting lets gravity do the work your hands have been fighting to do on every putt.
The first putter that works WITH the physics of your stroke instead of against them.
Stop compensating. Start making. RS1 eliminates the invisible force that drifts the face open.
headline: Your Putting Finally Matches the Rest of Your Game.
Do Less. Make More.
The Putter That Works With Gravity, Not Against It.
The Fight Between Your Hands and Your Putter Is Over.
What Happens When the Weight Is Finally in the Right Place.
hook: What if the putter — not your technique — was the problem?
There's a reason your practice-green putting doesn't transfer to the course.
Place this putter face-down on a table. Watch what happens.
Every putter you've owned had its weight in the same wrong place.
question: What if you could putt without fighting the putter on every stroke?
What if gravity actually helped your face stay square instead of pulling it open?
What would your score look like if your putting matched your ball-striking?
future_pace: Imagine standing over a 4-footer and knowing — not hoping — the face will track true.
Picture yourself halfway through a round and realizing you haven't thought about your putting stroke once. Just line, speed, roll.
Your playing partner asks what changed. You tell them: the weight is in the right place now.
benefit_bullet: Face stays square through impact — gravity pulls it toward the target, not away from it
Practice-green performance transfers to the course — the physics don't change under pressure
Natural pendulum stroke — the heavy front pulls the putter through, less hand manipulation required
Short putts become routine — the putter resists the face drift that causes pushes under pressure
The carousel stops — when the root cause is solved, you stop searching for the next putter
subject_line: The reason your practice putting doesn't transfer to the course
I stopped fighting my putter. Here's what happened.
Your hands have been doing a job the putter should have handled
What 75% of the weight in 25% of the head actually means for your score

variations_by_position.lead: Every putter you've ever owned has been fighting you. RS1 is the first putter engineered to fight FOR you — using gravity to keep the face square so your hands don't have to.
variations_by_position.thesis_statement: The FIRST putter engineered to work WITH gravity — not against it — so your putting finally matches the rest of your game.
variations_by_position.close: Move the weight forward. The fight is over. Your putting was never the problem — the putter was. RS1 fixes the physics. You make the putts.

dimension_scores.proof_ceiling_notes: No specific outcome numbers claimed. All claims grounded in physics (face angle consistency) and mechanism (Forward Axis Weighting reversal). Promise is about what the physics deliver, not fabricated test results.
dimension_scores.mechanism_fit_notes: Promise is precisely what Forward Axis Weighting delivers: face stays square because gravity pulls it toward the target line. The mechanism makes the promise physically true.
dimension_scores.schwartz_calibration_notes: Stage 4 audience needs a NEW mechanism to believe a new promise. The promise is calibrated to Forward Axis Weighting's unique physics, not generic improvement claims. 'Do less. Make more.' works at Stage 4 because it reframes the effort equation rather than making bigger outcome claims.
dimension_scores.competitor_differentiation_notes: No competitor promises that the putter actively assists technique AND eliminates equipment-induced face drift. LAB promises face stability but not stroke assistance. Traditional putters promise neither. 'Do less. Make more.' occupies genuine whitespace.
dimension_scores.specificity_notes: Promise is specific to face angle consistency, practice-to-course transfer, and pressure performance. Avoids vague 'better putting' claims. The 'match the rest of your game' framing is specific to the ball-striker-who-can't-putt persona identified in research.
dimension_scores.believability_notes: The Let It Fall demo makes the promise physically verifiable before purchase. The mechanism explanation (forward CG reverses gravitational torque) is scientifically credible. McGinley's credentials authenticate the engineering.
dimension_scores.emotional_resonance_notes: Directly addresses the Technique Deflection (ELG-01, magnitude 0.85) — vindication that the equipment, not technique, was the problem. Also addresses the Scorecard Split (UE-03) — the promise that putting will finally match ball-striking resolves the core identity tension.

primary_proof.element: Face Down Balance / Let It Fall demo — place RS1 on a flat surface, face sits down. Every other putter face sits up.
primary_proof.connection: If the face naturally falls toward the target line at rest (gravity), it naturally tracks toward the target line in motion (putting stroke). The demo proves the promise physically.

supporting_proof.element: Chris McGinley — 25+ years at major OEMs, clubs for 11 world #1 players
supporting_proof.connection: The engineer who designed for the world's best says Forward Axis Weighting is the most important innovation he's created. Authority validates the promise.

supporting_proof.element: 75%+ of 360g head weight in the front 25% of the head
supporting_proof.connection: Specific, measurable weight distribution that creates the gravitational advantage. Not marketing language — engineering specification.

supporting_proof.element: CG ahead of shaft axis creates face-squaring gravitational torque vs. CG behind shaft creating face-opening torque
supporting_proof.connection: The promise is grounded in basic Newtonian mechanics, not proprietary claims that can't be verified.

supporting_proof.element: LAB eliminates twist but leaves player alone. RS1 eliminates drift AND assists the stroke.
supporting_proof.connection: Evolutionary narrative proves RS1 goes further than the current category leader.

usage_guidance.notes: The full primary promise ('Your putting finally matches the rest of your game') works best as thesis and close. The tagline ('Do less. Make more.') works as a headline, ad hook, or email subject. The product truth line ('Gravity-Driven. Face-Controlled.') works as a product page section header.

disclaimers_required: Individual results vary based on stroke mechanics and green conditions.
RS1 addresses face angle consistency through Forward Axis Weighting. It does not guarantee specific scoring improvements.

platform_restrictions.restriction: Avoid before/after score claims without substantiation. Use 'designed to' language for performance claims.
platform_restrictions.compliant_version: Designed to keep the face square through impact using Forward Axis Weighting technology.

platform_restrictions.restriction: Avoid superlative claims ('best putter') without third-party verification.
platform_restrictions.compliant_version: The first putter with 75%+ of head weight forward of the shaft.

core_statement.full: RS1 doesn't just give you better technology — it actively helps your putting technique. Forward Axis Weighting creates a natural pendulum motion. The 74-degree lie angle reduces your stroke arc. The putter does more work so you do less.
core_statement.core: The putter does more work so you do less.

supporting_promise_packages.proof_pairing: Dual promise mechanism (technology + technique assist). No other putter addresses both pillars. McGinley call transcript confirms technique assist was designed into the product.
supporting_promise_packages.usage: Body copy mechanism section, product feature descriptions, comparison with LAB Golf

core_statement.full: Under pressure, your grip tightens and your hands can't compensate fast enough. With RS1, there's nothing to compensate for. Gravity handles the face. Your hands just guide the stroke.

supporting_promise_packages.proof_pairing: Root cause: pressure degrades hand compensation. Forward Axis Weighting removes the need for compensation. Physics don't change under pressure. Research quotes: Q0085 (shaking over every putt), Q0681 (visibly shaking on 16th hole), Q0782 (horrifying subconscious correction).
supporting_promise_packages.usage: Emotional body copy, pressure-putt narrative section, ad creative targeting competitive golfers

core_statement.full: When the root cause is solved, the search stops. RS1 addresses the one thing every putter you've owned had in common — the weight in the wrong place. Fix the weight, end the carousel.
core_statement.core: Fix the weight. End the carousel.

supporting_promise_packages.proof_pairing: Root cause: all previous putters had Rear Axis Weighting, so all produced the same face drift pattern. RS1 addresses the common root cause. Research quotes: Q0874 ('at least 20 different putters'), Q1318 (LAB user surprised putter stayed in bag), carousel denial latent emotion (ELG-03, magnitude 0.72).
supporting_promise_packages.usage: Email sequence, retargeting ads, objection handling for 'just another putter' skeptics

core_statement.full: Your practice-green putting is better than your on-course putting because pressure changes your hands, not your line reading. RS1 eliminates the variable that pressure exploits. What you can do on the practice green, you can do on the course.
core_statement.core: What you can do on the practice green, you can do on the course.

supporting_promise_packages.proof_pairing: Root cause: hand compensation degrades under pressure, creating the practice-to-course gap. Forward Axis Weighting removes the compensation requirement. Research quotes: Q0060 (practices hours daily, mixed results), root cause compressed copy block ('the practice green lies to you').
supporting_promise_packages.usage: Lead copy, email hooks, social proof integration point

core_statement.full: You're a better golfer than your scorecard says. Your ball-striking proves it. RS1 closes the gap between the golfer everyone sees at the range and the score that follows you off the course.
core_statement.core: Close the gap between your ball-striking and your score.

supporting_promise_packages.proof_pairing: Scorecard Split latent emotion (UE-03, magnitude 0.85). Research quotes: Q0208 ('people say I must be scratch, I say no I'm off 11 I can't putt'), Q0353 ('striking like a scratch, putting like a 12'). The promise resolves the core identity tension identified in the latent resonance field.
supporting_promise_packages.usage: Emotional lead variation, email sequence, retargeting creative, testimonial framing

primary_thesis.statement: The FIRST putter engineered to work WITH gravity — not against it — so your putting finally matches the rest of your game.

components.promise: your putting finally matches the rest of your game
components.mechanism: engineered to work WITH gravity — not against it
components.villain: every other putter works AGAINST gravity (Rear Axis Weighting creates face drift)

primary_thesis.formula: [FIRST] way to [match your putting to your ball-striking] is with [a putter that works WITH gravity]

runners_up.statement: The ONLY putter that eliminates face drift AND assists your natural stroke — so you do less and make more.
runners_up.why_not_primary: Strong on dual promise but 'ONLY' is harder to defend than 'FIRST' since Axis1 exists in the forward CG space (minimal market presence). 'FIRST' at scale with consumer narrative is defensible.

runners_up.statement: Move the weight forward. The fight is over. The first putter where gravity works for you, not against you.
runners_up.why_not_primary: Strong emotional close but functions better as campaign tagline sequence than as a formal thesis statement. Used in close position instead.

variations.headline_version: The First Putter That Works With Gravity.
variations.subhead_version: Forward Axis Weighting reverses the physics that drift your face open — so your putting finally matches the rest of your game.
variations.body_intro_version: For the first time, a putter is engineered to work with the laws of gravity instead of against them. RS1's Forward Axis Weighting puts the weight where it should have been all along — in front of the shaft — so the face tracks toward square instead of drifting open.
variations.close_version: Move the weight forward. The fight is over.

competitive_positioning.unique_claim: First putter to address both technology (eliminates face drift) and technique (assists natural pendulum stroke) through Forward Axis Weighting.
differentiated_from: LAB Golf: Zero torque eliminates twist but leaves player alone. RS1 eliminates drift AND assists the stroke.
Scotty Cameron: Premium craftsmanship and feel. No mechanism claim. RS1 claims specific physics advantage.
TaylorMade Spider: High MOI stability. Saturated claim (staleness 10/10). RS1 claims Forward Axis Weighting — different axis entirely.
Odyssey: AI-designed face. Failed mechanism at Stage 4 (staleness 6/10). RS1's mechanism is physically demonstrable.

copy_integration_guide.format_notes: Apple/VanMoof style product page. DR argument structure with premium branded execution. Not VSL, not long-form sales letter. Scrolling sections with generous whitespace.

section_instructions.hero_section: Primary promise headline + product truth line + hero image. 'Your Putting Finally Matches the Rest of Your Game.' / 'Gravity-Driven. Face-Controlled.' / RS1 hero shot
section_instructions.problem_section: Root cause revelation using copy blocks from root-cause-package.yaml. 'That miss wasn't mental. It was mechanical.' Lead with feeling, not physics.
section_instructions.mechanism_section: Forward Axis Weighting explanation with Let It Fall demo visual. Three-layer explanation (simple → medium → technical). Hammer analogy.
section_instructions.proof_section: McGinley credentials, 75%/25% spec, FDB demo, 10-piece construction, evolutionary narrative (old putters → LAB → RS1).
section_instructions.promise_section: Primary promise with future pacing. Supporting promises as feature-benefit pairs. 'Do less. Make more.' as section anchor.
section_instructions.offer_section: Product specs, two SKUs, Founders 500 positioning. Price anchoring vs Scotty Cameron and LAB Golf.
section_instructions.close_section: Emotional close: 'Move the weight forward. The fight is over.' Identity resolution: close the gap between ball-striking and score.

transitions.root_cause_to_mechanism: But what if the weight didn't have to be in the wrong place?
transitions.mechanism_to_promise: That's what changes when the weight moves forward.
transitions.promise_to_offer: RS1. The first putter that works with gravity.
transitions.offer_to_close: The weight is in the right place. The question is whether you are ready to feel the difference.

before_writing: Load Soul.md and apply voice constraints (quiet power, earned authority, no hype)
Load root-cause-package.yaml for copy blocks and anchor phrases
Load mechanism-package.json for Forward Axis Weighting hierarchy and proof elements
Confirm all claims respect proof ceiling (no fabricated outcome data)

during_writing.check: Every promise claim connects to mechanism
during_writing.how: Trace each promise to Forward Axis Weighting, FDB, or 74-degree lie angle
Lead with unique mechanism (Forward Axis Weighting) before making promise claims. The mechanism makes the promise believable.
No 'game-changing,' no hype, no dumbed-down science, no competitor bashing, no fabricated claims

after_writing: Tiger Test: Would every claim survive scrutiny from a tour pro or club designer?
Proof ceiling check: Are all outcome claims grounded in physics and mechanism, not fabricated data?
Soul.md check: Does the copy feel like quiet power + earned authority, not infomercial energy?
before_publishing: Chris McGinley review for technical accuracy
Compliance review for FTC and platform policies

resonance_connection.primary_promise_latent_emotion: The Technique Deflection (ELG-01) — vindication that equipment, not technique, was the problem

fssit_alignment.fssit_candidate: I knew something was wrong with the putter but I couldn't say it because golf says it's always the golfer's fault

supporting_promises_resonance.latent_emotion: The Pressure Fracture (UE-06) — sudden collapse of motor control at pressure threshold
Carousel Vertigo (UE-05) — meta-awareness of being trapped in repetitive pattern
The Scorecard Split (UE-03) — two contradictory identities in one golfer

frames_boosted_by_rsf: vindication (+3 from ELG-01 Technique Deflection alignment)
freedom (+2 from ELG-03 Carousel Denial alignment)
emotional_frame_rsf_influence.rsf_contribution_notes: RSF data strongly validated vindication as the primary emotional frame. The Technique Deflection gap (magnitude 0.85) represents the most powerful unspoken audience emotion: they SENSE the equipment is the problem but cannot say it. The primary promise crystallizes this into 'Your technique was never the problem.' This is a direct FSSIT delivery.

temporal_fit.timing_notes: Zero torque is rapidly becoming saturated (staleness 7/10, used by 5 brands). Forward Axis Weighting enters the conversation at the optimal moment — just as golfers are becoming skeptical of the current mechanism wave. RS1's promise arrives when the audience most needs a new framework.

to_big_idea.campaign_thesis: The FIRST putter engineered to work WITH gravity — not against it — so your putting finally matches the rest of your game.
to_big_idea.primary_promise_constraints: Promise grounded in face angle consistency and technique assist. No specific outcome numbers. 'Do less. Make more.' is the energy.
to_big_idea.emotional_foundation: Vindication (primary), Freedom (secondary), Control (tertiary). The audience finally gets permission to blame the equipment.
to_big_idea.differentiation_positioning: First putter to address both technology and technique through Forward Axis Weighting. Evolutionary, not adversarial.

to_structure.promise_positions: Hero headline, thesis statement, section anchor, close. Tagline throughout.
to_structure.proof_by_section: Problem section: root cause. Mechanism section: Forward Axis Weighting + FDB demo. Promise section: primary + supporting promises with proof pairings. Close section: emotional resolution.
to_structure.integration_instructions: Promise threads through entire page. 'Do less. Make more.' appears at least 3 times in different contexts.

to_offer.dream_outcome: Putting that matches ball-striking ability. Consistent face angle under pressure. End of putter carousel.
to_offer.transformation_arc: FROM: frustrated ball-striker bleeding strokes on the green, fighting invisible physics. TO: complete golfer whose putting matches their tee-to-green game.
to_offer.value_equation_inputs: High dream outcome (score reflects true ability), high likelihood (physics are demonstrable), low time delay (works immediately, no adjustment period needed), low effort (putter does the work).
